join_order.build: num(uom='pure', value NOT NULL) grouped by (adsh_code, tag_code) → max_value
join_order.notes: Pass 1: thread-local open-addressing hash maps, key=packed_uint64(adsh_code<<32|tag_code), value=double max_value; cap=65536 per thread (full cardinality, C9); sequential merge into global map after barrier. Pass 2: probe global map with same key, check n.value == max_value (exact double equality — both from same stored double column, C29 N/A for MAX).

join_order.probe: num rows passing max_value equality check
join_order.notes: Use pre-built indexes/sub_adsh_hash.bin (P11). cap=262144, slot={int32_t adsh_code, uint32_t row_idx}. After index lookup: check sub_fy[row_idx]==2022. Probe pattern: h=(adsh_code*2654435761u)&mask; bounded for-loop probe<cap (C24); sentinel=INT32_MIN (C20: use std::fill not memset). Only 2MB — fits in L3 cache, zero build cost.

filters.notes: Load num/uom_dict.txt at runtime, find pure_code via find_code() — NEVER hardcode (C2). ~39K rows pass.
null_fraction=0.0 per Query Guide — skip null check entirely.
Applied after sub_adsh_hash lookup: check sub_fy[row_idx]==2022. ~14.6K sub rows qualify.

maxval_map.merge_note: T×G = 64×39K ~2.5M; but global map is only 1MB (cache-friendly); sequential merge acceptable

output_collection.key_order: value DESC, name_code ASC, tag_code ASC
output_collection.note: Each thread maintains min-heap of size 100. At merge: collect up to 64×100=6400 candidates, partial_sort, take top 100 (P6)

passes.name: build_maxval_map
passes.scan: num (all 39,401,761 rows via morsel-driven parallel scan)
passes.action: For each qualifying row: key=packed(adsh_code,tag_code); probe thread-local map; if key exists and value[i] > stored max → update; else insert with value[i]
passes.parallelism: morsel_driven, 64 threads, each with thread-local hash map cap=65536
passes.post_pass: Sequential merge: for each thread-local map slot with key!=UINT64_MAX, update global map with max()

passes.name: probe_and_collect
passes.scan: num (all 39,401,761 rows via morsel-driven parallel scan)
action: 3. Probe sub_adsh_hash with adsh_code[i] → get sub_row
6. Insert into thread-local min-heap (size 100, keyed by value DESC)
passes.parallelism: morsel_driven, 64 threads, each with thread-local min-heap
passes.post_pass: Merge all thread-local heaps: collect ≤6400 candidates, std::partial_sort top 100 by (value DESC, name_code ASC, tag_code ASC), then decode strings

indexes.notes: 2MB pre-built hash index on sub.adsh. Eliminates runtime join build cost (P11). Probe: h=(adsh_code*2654435761u)&mask, bounded loop, sentinel=INT32_MIN.

aggregation_details.thread_strategy: thread_local_maps (cap=65536 each, full cardinality per C9)

decode: name: load sub/name_dict.txt at runtime; output name_dict[name_code] (C2, C18)
tag: load tag_global_dict.txt at runtime; output tag_dict[tag_code] (C2, C18)

correctness_checks.C2: Load uom_dict, name_dict, tag_global_dict at runtime via find_code(). Never hardcode.
correctness_checks.C9: maxval_map cap = next_power_of_2(39000*2) = 65536. Thread-local maps sized for FULL group cardinality.
correctness_checks.C15: Composite hash key includes BOTH adsh_code AND tag_code.
correctness_checks.C18: Decode name_code and tag_code to strings for CSV output.
correctness_checks.C20: Use std::fill for sentinel initialization (UINT64_MAX), not memset.
correctness_checks.C24: All hash map probes use bounded for-loop (probe < cap).
correctness_checks.C29: No SUM/AVG — MAX comparison with exact double equality is correct here.
